skill.name: popup-cro
skill.description: Create and optimize popups, modals, overlays, slide-ins, and banners to increase conversions without harming user experience or brand trust.
skill.summary: Design respectful popup strategies that improve conversions while protecting user experience, accessibility, privacy, and SEO.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but all reviewed locations are ordinary marketing, design, mobile UX, and CTA guidance. No evidence found of cryptographic code, system reconnaissance, scripts, network access, filesystem access, external commands, secret handling, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm

low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is visible metadata text only. There is no code or security-sensitive operation present.

content.value_statement: Poor popup timing and unclear offers can reduce conversions and damage trust. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design focused popup strategies with better triggers, copy, rules, and measurement.
actual_capabilities: Defines a single conversion goal for a popup, modal, overlay, slide-in, or banner.
Recommends trigger rules based on timing, scroll depth, exit intent, clicks, sessions, and behavior.
Drafts popup copy for headlines, subheadlines, calls to action, and decline options.
Adds UX safeguards for dismissal, mobile behavior, accessibility, privacy, and SEO.
Suggests frequency caps, targeting rules, exclusions, and test hypotheses.
limitations: Does not implement popup code or integrate with marketing platforms.
Does not replace legal review for privacy, consent, accessibility, or regional compliance.
Requires audience, offer, traffic, and performance context for precise recommendations.
Does not optimize the form fields inside the popup beyond basic guidance.

use_cases.title: Improve Lead Capture
use_cases.description: Create a focused email or lead magnet popup with clear value, respectful timing, and simple dismissal.

use_cases.title: Recover Abandoning Shoppers
use_cases.description: Design an exit intent offer with relevant copy, frequency limits, and checkout exclusions.

use_cases.title: Reduce Popup Friction
use_cases.description: Audit existing popup rules for mobile UX, accessibility, SEO risk, and user annoyance signals.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Popup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single recommendation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a popup recommendation for this page. Goal: [goal]. Audience: [audience]. Offer: [offer]. Include trigger, targeting, frequency, copy, design notes, and mobile behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Popup
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of current performance and user friction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this popup strategy and improve it. Current conversion rate: [rate]. Trigger: [trigger]. Complaint themes: [feedback]. Page type: [page]. Recommend changes and test hypotheses.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Multiple Popup Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced coordination across audiences and funnel stages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-popup strategy for [site type]. Define each popup purpose, trigger, audience, suppression rules, cooldowns, exclusions, copy direction, and primary metric.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose CRO and Compliance Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit for user experience, SEO, privacy, and accessibility.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this popup program for conversion risk and compliance risk. Context: [context]. Flag timing, mobile, accessibility, privacy, SEO, targeting, frequency, and measurement issues.

output_examples.input: An online store wants an exit intent popup for first-time visitors who viewed a product but did not add to cart.
output: Use an exit intent modal only on desktop and a bottom slide-up on mobile after meaningful browsing.
Offer a clear first-order incentive and exclude checkout, cart, and returning converters.
Test a direct benefit headline against an objection-handling headline, with conversion rate as the primary metric.

output_examples.input: A SaaS blog wants to promote a guide without hurting reader experience.
output: Use a scroll-triggered slide-in after 40 percent depth or 45 seconds of active reading.
Keep the copy specific to the article topic and ask for email only.
Cap exposure to once per session and suppress after dismissal for at least seven days.

output_examples.input: A product team receives complaints that the newsletter popup blocks mobile users.
output: Replace the full-screen mobile popup with a dismissible bottom banner or slide-up.
Delay display until the user has engaged with content, and keep tap targets large.
Track close rate, time to close, and conversion rate before scaling the new rule.

best_practices: Start with one popup goal and match the offer to the visitor context.
Use engagement-based triggers and frequency caps before testing visual changes.
Include dismissal, accessibility, privacy, mobile, and SEO safeguards in every recommendation.
anti_patterns: Showing a popup immediately before the visitor understands the page value.
Using multiple competing calls to action inside one popup.
Blocking checkout, signup, or other critical conversion steps with promotional overlays.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create popup, modal, overlay, slide-in, and banner strategies for conversion optimization.

faq.question: Can it write popup copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft headlines, subheadlines, calls to action, decline copy, and value statements.

faq.question: Does it build the popup code?
faq.answer: No. It provides strategy, copy, targeting rules, and testing guidance, not production implementation.

faq.question: Does it support mobile popup decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes mobile behavior, tap targets, dismissal, and intrusive interstitial risk.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Useful inputs include goal, offer, page type, traffic source, audience, device split, current metrics, and complaints.

faq.question: Is this suitable for regulated markets?
faq.answer: It can raise privacy and consent considerations, but legal and compliance teams should review final campaigns.
